Overwhelmed and constantly
afraid of losing your thoughts?
Everyday you’re bombarded with information.
Your non-connected notes lead to missing context when
you need it.
That gets future-you into trouble.
Logseq helps you
turn this daily mess into structured information.
Gain clarity
in your everyday life:
Relationships
Daily Plan
Journaling
Data Control
Communicate better.
Stay on top of your
relationships, conversations, and meetings.
“I mostly use it for work: keeping
daily notes & meeting notes. It's the best solution I've found to managing my tasks
(and I only use a fraction of the features there).”
User Feedback
by
oskr
Via
Discord
A safe space for
your thoughts.
Designed to store your
interests, questions, ideas, favorite quotes, reminders, reading and meeting notes
easily and future-proof:
Open source
Free forever for personal use
Privacy first
You own your data locally forever

Markdown files

Customize look and feel
Logseq Sync
BETA
Always up-to-date notes
between all your devices.
With encrypted file syncing, you'll always have your
notes
backed up and securely available in real-time
on any device.
Whiteboards
BETA
A new canvas
for your thoughts.
Place any of your thoughts from the knowledge base
or new ones next to each other on an infinite canvas
to connect, associate and understand in new ways.
Think faster,
think better!
By thinking and writing with Logseq, you'll
gain confidence in what you know and
stop worrying about forgetting
anything
.

hits.title: Logseq: Privacy-First, Joyful Platform for Knowledge Management
Logseq – privacy-first, open-source knowledge sharing and management platform
After Obsidian and Logseq, I give Dendron a try
Let's build a decentralized social network together with Logseq
SilverBullet – note-taking Git-friendly alternative to Logseq and Obsidian
Show HN: Logseq – A roam like open-source plaintext note-taking app
Logseq Raises $4.1M to Accelerate Growth of the New World Knowledge Graph
Logseq – adding settings for self-hosted sync

analysis.name: Logseq
analysis.value_proposition: A privacy-first, open-source outliner that stores all notes as local plain-text files and links them into a personal knowledge graph.
analysis.ideal_customer: Knowledge workers, students, academics, writers, and developers who want full data ownership and a Roam-style outliner without cloud lock-in.

features.name: local-first storage
features.description: All notes stored as plain-text files on the user's own device; no mandatory cloud.
features.evidence_quote: You own your data locally forever

features.name: open-source codebase
features.description: Entire application is open-source and free for personal use.
features.evidence_quote: Open source / Free forever for personal use

features.name: markdown support
features.description: Notes are stored as standard Markdown files, readable by other tools.
features.evidence_quote: Document formats: Markdown and Org Mode

features.name: org-mode support
features.description: Alternative document format using Emacs Org Mode syntax.
features.evidence_quote: Document formats: Markdown and Org Mode

features.name: daily journals
features.description: Built-in daily journal page for capturing time-stamped notes and tasks.
features.evidence_quote: Getting started with the Journals page

features.name: backlinks
features.description: Automatic reverse links shown on any page that is referenced elsewhere.

features.name: linked references
features.description: Panel showing all pages/blocks that link to the current page.
features.evidence_quote: Capture, structure, and review all of your class notes with ease using the Linked References

features.name: unlinked references
features.description: Surfaces mentions of a page that have not yet been formally linked.

features.name: block references
features.description: Embed or reference any individual block by ID across the graph.

features.name: block embeds
features.description: Inline live-rendering of another block's content inside the current page.
features.evidence_quote: The difference between block embeds and block references

features.name: page embeds
features.description: Inline live-rendering of an entire page's content inside another page.
features.evidence_quote: The difference between block embeds and page embeds

features.name: page aliases
features.description: Define alternate names for a page so links to either resolve to the same node.

features.name: properties
features.description: Key-value metadata attached to pages or blocks for structured data.

features.name: task management
features.description: Built-in TODO/DONE task states with priorities and scheduling on any block.

features.name: queries
features.description: Datalog-powered queries to surface and aggregate blocks matching any criteria.
features.evidence_quote: Use queries to generate tables of relevant information.

features.name: query builder
features.description: Visual UI for constructing queries without writing raw Datalog.

features.name: advanced queries
features.description: Full Datalog query language for complex cross-graph data retrieval.

features.name: templates
features.description: Reusable block/page templates that can be inserted on demand.

features.name: pdf highlights
features.description: Annotate PDFs directly inside Logseq with highlights linked back into the graph.

features.name: whiteboards
features.description: Infinite canvas for placing and connecting notes, blocks, and new content spatially.
features.evidence_quote: Place any of your thoughts from the knowledge base or new ones next to each other on an infinite canvas

features.name: flashcards
features.description: Spaced-repetition flashcard system built on graph content for memorization.
features.evidence_quote: Remember facts and insights using the Flashcards feature.

features.name: knowledge graph view
features.description: Visual node-link graph showing relationships between all pages in the graph.

features.name: plugins
features.description: Community plugin ecosystem (150+) for extending core functionality.
features.evidence_quote: 150+ Plugins / Extend functionality to your needs

features.name: custom themes
features.description: 30+ community-built themes to customize the UI look and feel.

features.name: search
features.description: Full-text search across all pages and blocks via CMD-K shortcut.
features.evidence_quote: Use CMD-K to search with ease.

features.name: encrypted sync
features.description: End-to-end encrypted cloud sync (Logseq Sync) keeping notes up-to-date across devices.
features.evidence_quote: With encrypted file syncing, you'll always have your notes backed up and securely available in real-time

features.name: self-hosted sync
features.description: Option to configure a self-hosted sync server instead of Logseq's cloud.
features.evidence_quote: Logseq – adding settings for self-hosted sync

features.name: mobile apps
features.description: Native iOS and Android apps for capturing and reading notes on mobile.
features.evidence_quote: Mobile apps / Available for iOS & Android

features.name: slide presentation
features.description: Convert a page's outline into a slide deck presentation.

features.name: calculator
features.description: Inline calculator blocks for arithmetic within notes.

features.name: tables
features.description: Structured table creation within pages.

features.name: draw
features.description: Embedded freehand drawing/sketching tool within notes.

features.name: media embeds
features.description: Embed audio, photos, and video files directly in note blocks.

features.name: zotero integration
features.description: Native integration with Zotero reference manager to pull citations and PDFs.

features.name: publishing
features.description: Publish a Logseq graph or subset of pages as a public website.

features.name: keyboard shortcuts
features.description: Comprehensive keyboard shortcut system for navigation and editing.

features.name: right-hand sidebar
features.description: Persistent sidebar panel for opening linked pages and blocks alongside the main view.
features.evidence_quote: How to work with the right-hand sidebar

features.name: commands
features.description: Slash-command and advanced command palette for inserting content and triggering actions.

features.name: hiccup
features.description: Clojure-style Hiccup syntax for writing raw HTML-like structures inside notes.

features.name: localization
features.description: UI translated into many languages by the community.

features.name: indentation / outliner
features.description: Hierarchical block outliner with infinite nesting via indentation.
features.evidence_quote: What is indentation and why does it matter?

features.name: linked references filtering
features.description: Filter the linked references panel by page, tag, or property to narrow context.

features.name: plugin api
features.description: Public plugin development API enabling community-built extensions.
features.evidence_quote: Create a plugin! Start with Plugins 101

features.name: existing markdown graph import
features.description: Open an existing folder of Markdown files as a Logseq graph without migration.
features.evidence_quote: How to create a Logseq graph using existing Markdown files

differentiators: Privacy-first: all data stored locally as plain-text files, no mandatory cloud
Open-source codebase — free forever for personal use
Markdown/Org Mode files openable in any other tool
End-to-end encrypted sync (Logseq Sync) with self-hosted option
Outliner-first structure with bi-directional linking at block granularity
Raised $4.1M to build a 'new world knowledge graph'

recent_ships.what: Whiteboards (BETA) — infinite canvas for spatial note connections
Logseq Sync (BETA) — encrypted cross-device file syncing

recent_ships.source: HN: 'Logseq – adding settings for self-hosted sync'

recent_ships.what: $4.1M seed raise to accelerate the knowledge graph platform
recent_ships.source: HN: 'Logseq Raises $4.1M to Accelerate Growth of the New World Knowledge Graph'

praised: Daily notes and meeting notes workflow: 'I mostly use it for work: keeping daily notes & meeting notes. It's the best solution I've found to managing my tasks' (oskr via Discord)
Privacy-first local storage praised heavily on HN (276↑ on privacy-first announcement post)
Roam-like outliner with bi-directional links for free
complaints: Performance / complexity noted as a friction point compared to alternatives like Obsidian (HN thread: 'After Obsidian and Logseq, I give Dendron a try')
User notes using only 'a fraction of the features' — feature surface seen as overwhelming for some
Sync reliability questions (self-hosted sync settings thread on HN suggests cloud sync needed work)

free_tier.limits: unlimited local graphs; personal use; no seat or storage cap

tiers.name: Personal (Free)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Full desktop + mobile app, all core features, local storage, open-source forever

tiers.name: Logseq Sync
tiers.headline_inclusions: Encrypted cross-device sync (BETA); price not publicly disclosed in scraped sources

pricing.notes: Pricing page was not scraped; Logseq Sync is in BETA and pricing details are unknown from available sources. Core app is free/open-source with no seat limits. A self-hosted sync option exists for users who want to avoid Logseq's cloud entirely.

recent_hn.title: Logseq Doctor: Heal your flat old Markdown files before importing them to Logseq
Logseq is splitting into two versions
Show HN: Slatewave – a single color palette across terminals, editors, and apps
